plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and income (QQQI) exposure.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to ease concentration.
Add defensive healthcare (XLV) and stable software (CRM) for quality.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
Expect lower volatility and a modest return target.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, focusing on stable assets and cash, leading to lower potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and income (QQQI).
Reduce semiconductor (SMH) and broad index (SPY) exposure.
Increase strong tech (CRWD) and add promising software (DDOG).
This balanced approach seeks growth while managing risk.
Leverage is kept low, and concentration is reduced.
Targeting a moderate monthly return with controlled drawdowns.
balanced.read: The balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% monthly return by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and a small allocation to leveraged ETFs, aiming for risk-first growth with moderate drawdowns.

plan: Increase leveraged semiconductor ETF (SOXL) exposure.
Sell income (QQQI) and other leveraged ETF (SPAL).
Trim broad index (SPY) but maintain semiconductor (SMH).
Add to strong tech (CRWD) and buy top software setup (ZS).
This aggressive plan concentrates on high-growth tech and leverage.
Expect higher potential returns but also significantly larger drawdowns.
aggressive.read: The aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly return by concentrating on high-conviction tech and increasing leveraged ETF exposure. This strategy embraces higher risk, including larger drawdowns and the daily rebalancing decay of leveraged ETFs, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (CRWD, SMH).
Significantly increase income ETF (QQQI) allocation.
Trim broad index (SPY) and add financial sector (XLF).
This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income.
Leverage is eliminated, and volatility is minimized.
Expect lower growth but prioritize capital preservation and yield.
income.read: The income plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and capital preservation, targeting around 1% monthly return. It shifts heavily into income-generating ETFs, cuts leverage and volatile single names, and maintains a defensive posture with minimal drawdown potential.
